messages.content: Here be the stage...
*steps onstage, goes to adjust mic, picks up a small bit of trash from the stage*
ahh, good thing I have a whole hour left to get this opening speech ready. Talk about waiting until the last minute, though. Whew. 😅
*hears whoops and cheers, and whirls around to find entire audience here and staring*
Oh…um…you guys sure are here early… right?
Please tell me you’re early and I didn’t screw up my time zone math again! 🥴🫠
Well, okay, um… so…
I guess we’ll just wing it. I took a single improv class 15 years ago, I should be fine… right?
*freezes for a moment to think*
Oh good, someone gave me coffee and a brain. 😅
I’ve been dealing with a whole heck of a lot recently. I’m beyond fortunate to have an amazing and accepting support system.

Of course an important part of that support system is FoLCdom. And actually, it's always been an important part of my support system since I was was... I don't do time math well... since pretty much the beginning of this fandom or close to it.
I have lifelong fandom friends. This fandom taught me how to hone my writing, how to edit, how to have fun and lean on my friends when life was rough as hell.
And you'll have to excuse me actually winging most of it here, hence the typing now rather than pasting 😅

But yeah, just when I’d gotten the hang of things— finding and respecting my limitations with chronic pain, settling into a late adhd diagnosis and figuring out habits and routines that help me work with my neurosparkly brain, and there was probably a third thing but I forgot. 😆
And yes, I know this is happy time, I'm bringing it back around, promise! 😆
A few months ago, life decided that it should hand me a whole new set of rules. 🫠
But you know what?

FoLCdom is still here. Y'all let me pop in and out and do what I can, when I can. Especially the rest of the K-Com. Because try being in charge of fandom awards when you're going through it and have brain fog and chronic pain flares plus all the real life things hitting too...
Sorry, I digress...
The K-Com, most especially @JadedEvie and @Darth_Michael did the lion's share of things, ~~forcing me~~ encouraging me to delegate. And... pretty much let me know that...
whatever I can do, that's enough 🥹 💜
So thanks for Lois'ing me. And also thanks to the fandom for still being here and stronger than ever after three decades. 🥹
*pauses awkwardly to go find the spreadsheet with the agenda...*
Ah, yes! Our first category!
I'd like to welcome @QueenoftheCapes to the stage (whose presentation will be pasted in by @Verity (Puffytiger)) for Best Micro-Short Story (1-5kb)
Hello everyone! I'm so excited to be here today
As long as I've been in fandom, this is the first Kerth Awards I've attended...and I'm presenting in it, haha
So, our first category is Best Micro-Short Story (1-5kB or up to ~ 893 words)
And @QueenoftheCapes appears to have written another one just for the presentation

On with it!

[Welcome to your Free Demo of LexWrite(TM), the ultimate software for writers.  Some features may be disabled.]

Ah, micro shorts.  What can be said about this genre of short form fiction?  Writing is already a daunting task that requires some level of talent, creativity, skill, and determination.  But when one limits the number of words that one can use, as is the case in short works, and especially micro shorts, how does one still manage to convey a complete thought, let alone a narrative?  Economy of words and pruning of ideas are thus, themselves, additional skills that are especially worthy of celebration.

[Error: Word count exceeded]

Writing a story takes talent, creativity, and skill.  This is especially true of short form works, like micro shorts, where conveying a complete thought must be accomplished in fewer words.

[Error: Word count exceeded]

Writing a complete story in few words, like micro shorts, requires talent and skill.

[Error: Word count exceeded]

Micro shorts take talent.

[Text approved.  Please subscribe to the full version of LexWrite(TM) to unlock unlimited word count, numerals, and additional punctuation.]
I also applaud her! Very well done.
and now, the nominees for Best Micro-Short Story are...
Computer Troubles by Queen of the Capes – 1 kB (149 words)
(did she channel her Kerth nomination for the intro???)
Lights and Music by bakasi – 4 kB (702 words)
Pancakes and Power Outages by 90stvfangirl – 3 kB (419 words)
And the winner is... (drum roll)
Lights and Music by bakasi !!!
Congratulations!!!
*looks around to see if @bakasi is here to give her speech...*
Ah, not currently, but she'll hopefully be able to pop in later!
*tucks first pilfered Kerth Award in her pocket and dusts off her hands*
Okay what's next...
Ah, yes, next we have Best Revelation presented by @Blueowl and pasted in by @CarrieRene
Hello, is this thing on? Just call me Grams now. I'm kidding, I think.
Here I stand (actually sitting) today, to present for @Blueowl.

There can never be too many revelation stories. We all love the thrill that comes with the moment of Lois finding out the truth or the dawning realization of Clark learning Lois has known about Superman for awhile. With some fics, the suspense pulls at us for a sustained length of time, while in others the reveal comes without warning and it’s the fallout we love to pour over. Each fic comes with its own unique spin, and this year five have been nominated for 2026’s Best Revelation story.
The nominees for Best Revelation Story are:
Falling for You (2025SF for JadedEvie) by kSaraSara
A Whole New World (2025SF for BlindPassenger) by 90stvfangirl
Youngest Reporter (2025SF for 90sTVFanGirl) by Queen of the Capes

And the winner is...
(slowly opening the envelope)

Best Revelation
Falling for You (2025SF for JadedEvie) by kSaraSara
Oh my... now I have to think up more words? 😅  Gahhhh thank you, everyone!  *puts Kerth in other pocket*

I love having the ficathons because it's a sure-fire way to force my brain to write, and I usually love what comes of it! And I was super happy to be able to write for @JadedEvie, who in a previous year, wrote and AMAZING one with my prompts! I just want to add that I spent time looking for the perfect actual Elvis story to include, since one of Evie's prompts was for Perry to tell an Elvis story 😆
Thanks again, everyone!!!
Next up, we have Best Comedy Story presented by @Toomi and pasted in by @Idon'tknowwhatthatmeans5678
Hi hi!
So before I copy in I want to take this moment to - err arrgh, I can;t write, the likes etc are popping up where I'm typing!
Please applaue in silence
Thank you for having me in this fandom, I can't express how much I love it here. And apologies for my stupidly long avatar name. I feel I'm stuck with it now!
Right,
onto the award
Comedy
The ability to make us laugh, smirk, or chortle aloud while reading and playing out a moment in our minds is a wonderful gift to have.

The author provides the reader a moment of escape, a chance to find humour in moments and with characters we have come to know and love. It's a light-hearted reminder that fic, and life, isn't always serious and we can have moments of light-heartedness.
The nominees for Best Comedy Story are:
well this is awkward...
Cacophony by Idontknowwhatthatmeans5678 / SonicMessiah
welcome to my world 😅
Oh, sorry, were you expecting other nominations?
Nope by SuperBek
A Whole New World by 90stvfangirl
Youngest Reporter by Queen of the Capes
Hoping someone tells me what happens next as I look like a fool standing here
There's an envelope?
right,  I have it
And the winner is...
it's a tie!!

A Whole New World (2025SF for BlindPassenger) by 90stvfangirl,
Cacophony (2025SF for BlueOwl) by Idontknowwhatthatmeans5678 / SonicMessiah,
Youngest Reporter (2025SF for 90sTVFanGirl) by Queen of the Capes
three-way split!!
er, I since I'm here I'd just like to say THANKS!!!
I'm very honoured to share with these writers!

*tells @Idon'tknowwhatthatmeans5678 to keep her Kerth*
*pockets the other two*
*waves it above her head*
ah, since the other two aren't here to give speeches, we'll move on to the next category
(omg my dog is whining at me... and since I got my hubby to take the kid and leave the house for the morning... I have to let her out... *digs category info up quickly*)
next we have Best Kiss presented by @blg1987!
*Introducing our next category, please welcome to the stage blg1987 and the legendary Cat Grant!*
[Both women make their way on stage, beautiful ballgowns sweeping the ground. Blg1987 takes first position at the microphone.]

Blg1987: It is such a great honour to be here. Lois & Clark has been central to my life since I was a little kid, drawing a red S on a yellow t-shirt and running around with a towel tucked in for a cape.

The show taught me a lot over the years, it's messages of hope and heroism still shaping who I am today. But it also showed me what a healthy relationship can look like. One full of vulnerability, intimacy and-
[Cat nudges closer to the microphone]
Cat Grant: Sucking face
[Blg1987 glares at Cat, resuming position in front of the microphone]
Blg1987: And *kissing*. [Blg1987 eyes Cat warily]
Over the years, we got to see all manner of tender and loving moments of-
Cat Grant: Spit swapping
[Blg1987 pushes Cat back from the microphone, harder this time]
Blg1987: Moments of *romance*. We saw our favourite couple connecting after many heart felt talks, often closing the show with a suggestive fade to black as they-
Cat Grant: Played tonsil hockey
Blg1987: Oh for goodness sake, do you want to just take over?
[Cat smiles mischievously, then steps up to the microphone]
Cat: As my co-presenter was so politely saying, Lois and Clark have been seen locking lips so often it's a surprise the show aired on primetime TV!
We saw them necking in the newsroom. Tongue wrestling in the townhouse. Canoodling in the clouds. It's a wonder any of you fanfic authors had any ideas left for the two love birds to smooch.
Blg1987: That just shows how inspired they all were by the beautifully tender-
Cat: *cough* Steaming hot *cough*
Blg1987: -Relationship Lois & Clark embodied.
So why don't we remind our audience exactly what the writers nominated tonight were aiming to live up to.
Cat: [rubs hands together] It's about to get real in here

- BEST KISS VIDEO COMPILATION - 
*(the video contains a variety of short clips of Lois & Clark kissing, set to the song 'I Wanna Be Your Girlfriend' by Girl In Red)
(sound on if you have it, FoLCs... though it shouldn't *auto*play...)
[Cat fans herself]
Cat: Meow. I rest my case.
[Blg1987 rolls her eyes]
Blg1987: Without further ado - or any other inappropriate comments from my co presenter - let's see who has won this year's Kerth award for Best Kiss.

The nominees for the Best Kiss story are:
110 Kisses: This Isn't Fair by KSaraSara 
Fade to Black: Pilot (The Interview) by lovetvfan 
The Proposal by JadedEvie
Cat: And the winner is...
The Proposal by JadedEvie!!!!
Wow, thank you so much, everyone!

I’m earnestly so grateful to everyone who read this story, who beta’d this story, who nominated and voted, and especially everyone who left feedback, which I always read and obsess over, and end up writing more because of.
*slowly tries to reach for the 🏆 while JadedEvie is making her speech...*

Extra special thanks to 90stvfangirl who beta’d and was a tremendously helpful sounding board, and who watched as this went from a 70-page angst bomb to a 25-page romp to the waffy, daring, zany trip that it ended up becoming - with an iconic kiss, apparently!
This story felt like a big (and silly) swing for me.

I’ve got an awfully big case of imposter syndrome, something I know a lot of writers share (even here, where the authors are SO talented!). To be honest, thinking of myself as a writer still feels fake. I started writing out of grief, after my closest friend passed away - which is where my first ever story, Undercover Reporter, came from, where Lois gets her best friend back against impossible odds.

But then I kept going. Partly because of the addicting, serotonin-boosting feedback, partly because of the incomparably warm and welcoming community here, and partly as a way to process what emotions in what can feel like a very full brain.
The Kerth’s are important to me, and they go a long way toward pushing away that grief and imposter syndrome and letting in light and joy instead.
And The Proposal was some of that joy.
This is the first Kerth ceremony I've been able to make it to, but thank you for three years of Kerths! Thank you, again!
*wipes tears from eyes* 🥹
Emoji reacts :superman: if you've used fanfic for therapy! 🙋‍♀️
(another thing FoLCdom has given me!)
Congrats, @JadedEvie!
Up next, we have Best Super-Short Story presented by @Morgana and pasted in by @AnnaBtG
Hi all!
I'm sure Morgana would love to be here, but since she's not, let me don a witchy hat 🎩
_is now known as Morgana_
*Morgana steps up to the podium again and looks out into the audience and with an expansive wave of her hand clutching the all-important envelope says,*

I'm back guys. Ever had a long, boring day at work? We’ve all had them! You simply want to forget about endless e-mails, moving target deadlines and nosy co-workers gossiping over the cubicle wall. What to do? When lunchtime came I used to shut everything down on my computer, head to the cafeteria, pull out my iPad and start reading a Super Short Lois and Clark story destined to make this loyal FoLC smile or think of what a clever spin on a reveal!
Some of those stories were so good, so cute the memory of them had me smiling until quitting time! This year’s nominees have done just that!
*Morgana opens up the envelope, reads it, snickers and says:*

The nominees for Best Super-Short Story (5-10kb) are:
The Lab Incident by Blind Passenger
Nope by SuperBek
The Youngest Reporter by Queen of the Capes
And the winner is….
Youngest Reporter (2025SF for 90sTVFanGirl) by Queen of the Capes
Congrats @QueenoftheCapes !!!
*swipes 🏆 and puts in the pilfering pocket*
Any more of these, I think I might need to start stashing them behind the half-dead ficus instead... 🪴
congrats, @QueenoftheCapes!!
Next up, we have Best Episode Adaptation presented by @Idon'tknowwhatthatmeans5678
I'm back!
And as you can see better dressed
So ignore the wailing coming from the back of the stage
Cat will get her dress back later
On to the next award

As much as we FoLC love ‘Lois and Clark’ sometimes we think things on the show could have been a little different, or we wish we could have seen what happened next, or in between scenes (or seen Lois and Clark more than just kiss…).
We’d like to be privy to our favourite characters’ private thoughts onscreen. There are as many interpretations as there are writers and we’re lucky to read these imaginative ‘what ifs’.

This award celebrates the writers whose stories tinkered with canon in sometimes humorous and sometimes moving and sometimes horny ways, always with respect to the original material, remaining satisfyingly faithful to the characterisation and the episodes’ essential plotlines.
when everyone has calmed down we can move on to the The nominees for Best Episode Adaptation are:
oops, there was some typng and some pasting
Fade to Black: Pilot (The Interview) by lovetvfan
Girls Really Do Solve Everything by Darth Michael
IBM: Signs by JadedEvie
Something Worth Fighting For by Abigail Ellen Kent
And the winner is….
sorry, got lost on way back
Fade to Black: Pilot (The Interview) by lovetvfan
congrats, Twinnie!! Are you here to give us a speech?
ah, no answer to PM. I know weekends are busy family time for her!
*puts 🏆 in the other pocket with her first award...*

I know she'd be really thrilled to win! She loves the challenge of writing the Fade to Blacks and making them in character, no matter how early in the show the episode was... even the first episode! 😅
Up next, we have Best Challenge Response presented by @CarrieRene
Hello again. I'm here to present for Best Challenge. Of course, I find out about 10 minutes ago that it's a challenge to keep an almost 6 month old baby girl healthy. Once again Evelyn is sick.

Life is full of challenges — the daily kind, like what to eat, what to wear, what bill gets paid first. But in the world of fanfiction, a challenge takes on a whole different meaning.
This year’s nominees all come from the Summer Ficathon Challenge, where every participant received a secret prompt and wrote a story for another author. The fun wasn’t just in the writing — it was in trying to guess who wrote what, and watching how each writer transformed their prompt into something wonderful and their own.
Who doesn’t love those types of challenges! 
And every single one of them rose to the occasion.
The nominees for Best Challenge Story are

A Bird in Hand for KSaraSara by Blg1987,
But Who’s Got You? For AmandaK by LoveTVFan,
Cacophony For BlueOwl by Idon’tknowwhaththatmeans5678/SonicMessiah
Falling for You For JadedEvie by KSaraSara
And
Sleight of Hand for QueenoftheCaps by JadedEvie.

And the envelope please.... 
And the winner for Best Challenge is:
Sleight of Hand (2025SF for QueenoftheCapes) by JadedEvie
Y’all, I AM SO EXCITED for this one.  THANK YOU so much for loving this story as much as I did!
I was really not expecting this!
I loved writing it, from the angst to getting to play with Zatanna, to a fun and flirty and sweet happy ending.  Plus, it helped cure a huge writers’ block moment.
Huge thanks to Verity/PuffyTiger for organizing this whole thing, and for replying to my frantic messages when I was worried about hitting the deadline with a 90-page story!
And yes, a sequel is coming. 😈
So grateful to you all for this. Thank you!  💞💞💞
*holds up @JadedEvie's 🏆* you got this? Or need me to carry it for you...?
Naw, girl, I got these. 😉
Just trying to be :superman: super helpful!
Next up is Best Dramatic Story presented by @Verity (Puffytiger) which will be... you'll never guess... pasted in by @Verity (Puffytiger)
Yay! back again. And this time as myself
You all know I'm not a creative writer. I'm an excellent organizer and technical writer, but I lack the multitude of ideas you creative writers have. I'm in awe of all of you!
When I wrote my introductions for the categories, @JadedEvie said it was like a little glimpse of me as a creative writer, and that made me feel 🥹
So here we have the best dramatic stories!
Which is a little funny. I actually had to do research on what makes up a dramatic story...because in my mind, most of them are!
I'm glad I'm not the one who had to make those decisions, haha
But I definitely enjoy reading them!
One of the things I love about dramatic stories is the intensity of emotion that is often captured in them.
Angst, joy, misunderstanding, resolution... the characters going through these journeys help me to better understand my own journey through life and the people around me.
They give me new questions to ask myself about why people do the things they do, and even why I do the things I do.
@KSaraSara asked us earlier if anyone had used fanfic as therapy. And...it's a huge yes from me
It helped me work through a bunch of ugh, gave me a better understanding of what healthy relationships might look like

And helped me understand so much better what might be motivating those around me. It's made me a better person, and I'm Super grateful for everyone who has written these stories over all the years of this fandom!
And now on to what everyone is waiting for! The nominees are ....
A Bird in Hand by Blg1987
But Who's Got You? by LoveTVFan
Found by SuperBek
If You Only Knew by Carrie Rene
And the winner is...
But Who's Got You? by @lovetvfan !!!!
Congratulations!
I will hold the 🏆 so she doesn't get ice cream on it!
Is her internet (and butter making) going to let her make a speech?
Hey, there she is!
Got time to jump over here?
Omg!
I didn't think I was going to be able to come on and I honestly did not expect anything in this category there were so many GOOD stories!
@Verity (Puffytiger) thank you for your help and pushing me to get it done 😂
Seriously I'm really touched. I haven't ever written alt Clark before so thank you! *Exits before I get cut off again*
Alright, one more category before intermission!!
Next up is Best Short Story presented by @AnnaBtG … pasted in by @AnnaBtG lol
It's me again!
In the flesh!
It’s lovely to be back for another year of the Kerths!
I’m here to present Best Short Story, which is one of my favourite categories!

For those who don’t know me, I am not ashamed to admit that I do most of my L&C reading between breaks at work :p so those stories are the perfect length to get me through the day! Just enough to keep me entertained between customers, and not too long that I’ll have to part with them midway at the end of the working day! Really very considerate of y’all to write them, thank you!
The Best Short Story nominees are:

A Bird in Hand (2025SF for kSaraSara) by Blg1987
The Costume Party: A Choose Your Own Adventure Fic by Queen of the Capes
Falling for You (2025SF for JadedEvie) by kSaraSara 
Just Us This Time (2025SF for LoveTVFan) by Kathryn84
Sanctuary (2025SF for aPurpleOakie) by Kaylle
And the winner is...
It's a 👔 !!!

A Bird in Hand (2025SF for kSaraSara) by Blg1987 
&
Falling for You (2025SF for JadedEvie) by kSaraSara
Congrats, girls!!
@KSaraSara you can only take one!!!
ahhh holy moly! I've already said my piece on Falling for You, thank you so much 🥰
WHAAAAAAAT?
*hands mic over to @blg1987*
Thanks so much all! Funnily enough, when I wrote this I was worried it'd be too long for a gift exchange... 😅
I'll know better for next time!
Thanks to @KSaraSara for requesting a yellow warbler
(don't let @lovetvfan read that part of your speech 😆*)
The real star of the fic
Congrats, @blg1987!!
Congrats @KSaraSara
Okay, FoLCs, it's intermission time! (which, according to the spreadsheet is supposed to be 15 mins long...)
Hopefully someone besides me is doing the time math to know when it's been 15 mins...
2 minute warning
*pushes mic at @Darth_Michael*
No introduction?
oh my
_adjusts olde-fashioned straw hat_
Welcome back to the second half of the Kerth Awards ceremony!
_gesticulates with baton_
I hope ye all enjoy the morning-evening of today’s festivities!
We have cotton candy in the hall, live entertainment, and all the trappings of a professional circus!

You may remember last year’s hoopla with the clapping and the frogs raining from the heavens, so I shall refrain from clapping this year. We don’t need another lawsuit from the Rainforest Animals Protection Group eating into sparse funds again.
_puts baton down of podium and straightens blue vest_
Speaking of funds.
As you might know, our fandom runs on the generosity of its members.
_points at assembled FoLCs_
That’s *YOU* guys!
You donate your time and skill and love to the creation and appreciation of fanfiction.

And sometimes, you also donate your hard-earned money to help us pay for the resources needed to host the Boards, the Archive, the nfic Archive, and the Kerth site – all on one nice and compact system, all for the low, low price of just a buck and a quarter a day.
To make this possible, we pay for the server once every three years, getting us a hefty rebate. And the next payment is due in early 2027.
As it stands, we’re still about 800 dollars short of our goal and thus we’re going to be asking you all to help us make a fundraising event a reality.
We had those before, although the last one is more than a decade past, and they were always fun. We had swag. We had fics. We had art. We had auctions.
**And we had someone to organize the whole shindig.**
This is where one of you guys hopefully comes in. Sara and I try not to monopolize the fandom, so if someone enjoys organizing things, please let us know. We’d so very much love the help!

To this effect, @JadedEvie  created a post on the message boards with all the details: https://www.lcficmbs.com/ubb/ubbthreads.php/topics/304378#Post304378 Please check it out and contact us if you are interested in helping!

embeds.title: Funding the Boards and Archive - We need your help!
embeds.description: Hi FoLCs, As many of you know, we are funded through the end of 2026. But we’d love to have the community continue next year -- and the year after t...

messages.content: One last detail before I hand over the mic to our MC – please, please, please, understand that after what happened during the Fruit Fly Photoshoot of ’94, we’re forbidden by Human Resources and Legal from auctioning off dates with Cat or Superman. _That_ event resulted in so many lawsuits about sexual harassment, that our venerable Editor in Chief had to take up a second job as a fortune teller at carnivals, if you can believe it!

So, please remember, it’s okay to auction off Lois for your personal entertainment. It’s okay to auction off Clark for Lois’s entertainment. It is _NOT_ okay to auction off Superman to Lois _OR_ to men wearing tinfoil suits. I know this is an imposition, but please work with us on this restriction.
_points clicker at vid screen_
And if you need someone to work your kid’s party, Madame Velofksy’s fortunes are available for birthdays, Bat Mitzvas, as well as junior and senior proms.
_steps away from podium so @KSaraSara  can take over_
here, don't forget your complimentary gift! *hands @Darth_Michael a few 🐸*
Ooooh! now I need to find a fic to put them in...
Next up is Best Original Character/Supporting Character presented by @Idon'tknowwhatthatmeans5678!
Hiiii!
Okay okay, enough applause. I *may* have spilled ramen down Cat's dress. She doesn't need to know
So onto our first award of the second half

Lois and Clark are our favourite duo, but the show is blessed with a set of iconic supporting characters. With often limited screen time, fan fiction offers writers the freedom to explore these popular, occasionally contentious, characters and deepen FoLCS relationship with them.

Furthermore, our brilliant fan fiction writers create their own characters to embellish this fictional universe. It’s never easy to make us fall in love with an original character but these stories successfully did that.
These characters may take centre stage to shine, or they continue in a supporting role to Lois and Clark, and through their presence, show us different sides to the main characters.
The nominees for Best Original Character/Supporting character are:
Jamie Evans: But Who’s Got You? By lovetvfan
Mayson Drake: A Different Perspective by Krista/FragmentedSandwiches
Jonathan and Martha Kent: Found by SuperBek
And the winner is….
Jonathan and Martha Kent: Found by SuperBek
*snags another 🏆*

Bek is usually busy with mom stuff on the weekends, so I'm assuming she'll see this later. I was lucky to be able to read this as she was writing, and watching little Clark and Martha and Jonathan come to life and break my heart in so many ways was an honor. I know she'll be really really grateful to get this award because if you see how deeply Martha and Jonathan care for Clark here, that's how much Bek cares about her characters when she's writing them *and* long after that. 🫶
(and yep, she just replied saying she's busy chauffering kids)
*waits for someone to announce the next category*
kidding, I actually wrote this one ahead of time lol
Next up is Best Mid-Length Story presented by my Twinnie @lovetvfan and pasted in by @Verity (Puffytiger)  while my Twinnie eats ice cream and makes butter, apparently…
Prioritizing ice cream over Kerths? pssh
Maybe you should keep that first one she won in your pocket, @KSaraSara lol
Ok, here we have Best Mid-Length Story (51-200kB or ~9,000-36,000 words)
Anyone who knows fic writers, knows that we have difficulty with length. Like…a lot of difficulty.
Some of us write stories that are REALLY good but also REALLY long coughyouknowwhoyouarecough and then there’s the ones who write the absolutely delightful little fics and ficlets!
But like Goldilocks, the mid-length story has to be neither too short, nor too long in order to be just right.
Here are the nominees for best Mid-Length fic:
Lois: “Psst!”
Narrator: “What?”
Lois: “Your speech is a bit too short, don’t you think?”
Narrator: “What do you mean?”
Lois: “I just mean these are important stories. You should say a bit more about them – you know…REALLY give them the love they deserve.”
Narrator: “I thought I just did but what else do you want me to say?”
Clark: “Lois, come on I thought the speech was just fine. Short and sweet! Not everything has to be a novel.”
Lois: (suspiciously)  “What do you know about my novel?”
Narrator: “Umm, right. Anyway, here are the nominees for best Mid-Length fic”:
Fade to Black: Pilot (The Interview) by lovetvfan – 155 kB (29,402 words)
The Proposal by JadedEvie – 95 kB (16,522 words)
The Ruse by Socomama – 183 kB (32,739 words)
Sleight of Hand (2025SF for QueenoftheCapes) by JadedEvie – 137 kB (24,937 words)
And the winner is...
The Ruse by @Socomama !!!
Congratulations!!  Is she around to make a speech?
*puts 🏆 in pocket*
Guess not. Another Kerth for that pocket, @KSaraSara
Suppose not, shame...
Hey, I *DO* give them all to their proper recipients! 😅

🦉 Hello everyone!
(Because in all seriousness, I only want the Kerths I earn 🥰 )

There is just something about elseworld or AU stories that satisfies a craving we didn't even know we had. Whether our favorite characters are set in a universe with a single aspect different from canon – such as a critical event from canon occurring differently – or the entire setting has shifted into something entirely unrecognizable with our tried and true heroes navigating it, these stories provide us new worlds to dive into to the point we wonder which we prefer: them or canon.
This year, three have been nominated for 2026’s Best Elseworlds/AU story. The nominees for Best Elseworlds/AU story are:

A Bird in Hand (2025SF for kSaraSara) by Blg1987
But Who's Got You? (2025SF for Amanda K) by LoveTVFan 
Found by SuperBek

Next up is Best Waffy Story presented by @Morgana  and pasted in by @CarrieRene
Hello again.
I'm here to present for @Morgana

BEST WAFFY:

Morgana comes up to the podium, clears her throat and begins with a thoughtful
expression.

Greetings and Salutations fellow FoLC’s!
I’m here to talk about an interesting term we all know and love…
Waffy.

Upon first hearing this years ago when I first joined the fandom one thought came to mind, where did such an odd word come from? Is it real or made up? Is it a word Perry would consider using in a story while holding his red editor’s pen? Maybe Cat came up with it during some swanky society event? One thing is for sure, it ain’t a word Lex Luthor would use! (Morgana fans herself thinking of sexy Lexy in a bespoke double-breasted suit. She stops, remembers where she is and then continues.) Seeking an answer, I pulled out my trusty, well-thumbed thesaurus to look it up and what did it say?
Yikes! No such word was found.

That tells me it is exclusively FoLCs own special expression… used to describe sweet, serendipitous things and events regarding our favorite duo of intrepid reporters! Kudos to us!
Hey, does anybody out there know who came up with ‘waffy’? If so, please let me know!

That being said, the nominees for Best Waffy Story are:
Cravings by aPurpleOkie
Lights and Music by Bakasi
The Proposal by JadedEvie
The Ruse by Socomama

Morgana rips open the envelope starts jumping up and down and says:
And the winner is…

Best Waffy
The Ruse by Socomama
Woooo! congrats again, @Socomama!!! *carefully stuffs 🏆 in very full pocket*

This one is for Best Long Story (201+kB or ~36,000+ words)
When a person reads as fast as I do, many stories are just tasty little snacks.
Delicious, but fleeting.
A well written long story is like a drawn out meal with good friends, where we can spend time together and really savor the duration of quality time.
(unless I'm attempting to beta for guess the author, then I'm just reading through 45K words in about 3 hours, haha)
I did say I read fast!
A-hem, back to the presentation:
I love long stories just for this reason, they let me submerge myself into the characters and world. Dwell within the story, consider the motivations, learn from the experience.
Annnnnddddd... have it take a while before I have to figure out what I'm reading next!

I'm here.

Since I'm not very good at speeches, I invited a special guest for the presentation of the next award. Though why he volunteered for this particular category, I'll never know. Ladies and Gentlemen, please welcome H.G. Wells.
*Wells walks down the aisle and takes off his bowler head, before he climbs onto the stage and turns to the audience. He fumbles with an envelope.*

Wells:  “Thanks for having me. I feel very honored to be presenting this category. As you might know, I’m a writer myself and I have a lot of experience writing in this particular field. So I am pleased to announce this year's nominees for the science fiction category.”
bakasi_ “Uh, Mr. Wells, I’m deeply sorry, but I have a feeling you’re confusing this category with something else. It’s nfic, not science fiction, I’m afraid."

Wells: “You sure? It was raining when the postman brought my letter and the ink got wet in several places. So I assumed - are you certain this n here isn’t about science? What a pity. I was so looking forward to….”
bakasi: “Well, to be frank, the n stands for naughty.”
“Naughty, huh? But pray tell me, how on earth could a fanfic be naughty?”
bakasi: “Uh, you see…”
*Realization dawns on our good time-traveler’s face and he blushes all over.*

“Don’t worry, sweety,” *Cat Grant, dressed in a flimsy breath of nothing, appears on the stage, and grabs Well’s envelope, exchanging it for the one she’s carrying.* “I’ll gladly take over.  Someone -"  *Looks at bakasi with daggers in her eyes* "-thought it was a good idea to give me the category best overall. As if there was anything good about overalls!”
Bakasi “We’re not giving out awards for clothing, Cat. But nevermind, if you want to do the honors, by all means. "Be our guest.”

*Well’s, his face still the color of a ripe tomato, hurries to pull something from his pocket.* “Oh in that case, I hope no one minds, if I excuse myself for a moment.” *He presses a button on a small device and steps through an open time window.*

“Thanks. I’m much better suited for this category than anyone else, don’t you think?” *She pulls at the piece of clothing she’s almost wearing, making sure it shows all her attributes before stepping up to the mic.*

Next up one of my favorite categories to see because that means we’ve still got new writers and new folcs! There to present Best New Author is last year’s winner, @AmandaK (and hopefully she's available to paste her own speech)
Hello everyone!
I’m AmandaK, winner of last year’s Best New Author, and today I will be presenting this year’s Best New Author award.

I started writing fanfiction way back in middle school for a variety of other fandoms. It had been some years since Lois and Clark had gone off the air so it wasn’t really on my radar at the time. After years of writing, I set it aside for several years and I didn’t have any plans to pick it up again.

Then, one day, after re-watching all of Lois and Clark, I found myself wanting more stories (the series ended far too soon). So, I ventured into the world of fanfiction, expecting to find only a handful of fanfics.

Imagine my surprise when I discovered the archives with its plethora of prose going all the way back to the beginning. I was further surprised to discover the message board and a thriving community of FOLCS still churning out stories to keep us entertained for years to come.

I didn’t set out to start writing fanfiction again but, after a little while, I was pulled back in and I found a welcome community ready to provide encouragement and feedback. And we are still growing!
We have five nominees for Best New Author this year! I’ve read and enjoyed so many of their stories and I can’t wait to see what they write in the future.

Maybe they stumbled into our fandom while browsing other Superman fanfics. Maybe they are lifelong Lois and Clark lovers who didn’t know this community was here waiting for them until a random search pointed them in the right direction. Or perhaps they’ve been lurking in the background for years and finally found the courage to share their own works.
However they came to be here, we are all certainly glad they came!
So, without further ado, our nominees for Best New Author are:
Abigail Ellen Kent
Blg1987
Idontknowwhatthatmeans5678 / SonicMessiah
Krista / FragmentedSandwiches
aPurpleOkie
And the winner is...
Congratulations!

So I guess i'm a real writer now?

Best Commenter/Feedback Giver presented by @Kaylle and pasted by @Idon'tknowwhatthatmeans5678
Back for the final time!
Cat is getting cold in her teeny tiny slip of an outfit from a piece of curtain and wants her dress back so I'll be quick

Writing is often a lonely pursuit. In movies we see writers alone at their typewriters, or staring dreamily into the sunset as they scribble in a notebook, or typing thoughtfully on their laptops and drinking fancy coffees.

The reality of a fanfic writer is often even less glamourous. I write on my couch in my pyjamas in the middle of the night. Some of us write on our phones on trains and buses, or while juggling sleeping children. But it's still a solitary activity -- just a writer crafting their story, word by word, sometimes hard fought.

And when the chapter or the story is finished, we send it out into the silent ether, hoping it will be well received but never really knowing. Was it funny enough? Did that scene make sense? Was that change of heart believable? Does anyone want to see more of this story? Am I even any good at this writing thing?

And if it wasn't for kind and dedicated readers like those in our next category, we would still be wondering and worrying alone in our pyjamas on our couches. Feedback from readers is how a bunch of solitary writers become part of a fandom community.
Feedback tells us what we're doing right. "This was hilarious! That scene was so complicated but you nailed it! I never would have believed that character could change, but you made me believe it!"
Feedback helps us keep going on that long epic when we're losing steam. "I can't wait to see what happens in the next chapter!"

And feedback is what helps us grow as writers -- when readers take the time to tell us what is working for them, what they're enjoying, and how we're improving. "You are so good at this writing thing! This is my favourite story of yours so far!"

The nominees today are especially good at building that community between readers and writers in our fandom. They keep writers writing with their compliments, their energy, their excitement. Fandoms die with lack of engagement but FoLCdom is still going strong after thirty years, thanks to readers like these.
This year's nominees for Best Commenter/Feedback Giver are:
90stvfangirl
Bakasi
DarthMichael
JadedEvie
Songbird

“I hope our dear Mr. Wells used his time to prepare his speech for the next category.” *Eyes wristwatch. “He’s running a bit late.” *Fumbles with a few sheets of paper in her pocket. Let’s out a sigh of relief as a time window openes next to her. “Oh, Mr. Wells so glad to see you back here. But please tell me what happened to you?”

*H.G.Wells looks slightly deranged. His bowler hat sits askew and his bow tie hangs loosely around his neck. He sounds a little winded as he speaks. *Oh, I stumbled into the aftershow party to find out how the FoLCs were liking my speech. And then KSaraSara attacked me with a whole series of questions about the Alternative Universe, because she wanted to write a sequel to that story she wrote. And then there was Queen of the Capes asking me about some Ice world. Apparently, she wanted to know if they ever go back. I don’t really remember. I think AmandaK bugged me about an Elseworld where Lois and Clark got married to make peace between Earth and New Krypton.”
“Thanks, I think we get the picture. So what about your speech? I know you prepared for the nfic category, but Cat Grant took over and people are getting impatient.”

“Oh, don’t worry. One of the perks of being a time traveler is that you can take as much time as you need and still return precisely to the moment you’re needed again.” *He pulls a neatly folded envelope from his pocket.* “So this year, we had a total of seven nominations for the Best Overall category.
